evidence_quotes.quote: adding vague and often unclear terms of service.
Partners of those platforms no longer have a clear guidance what they can and cannot offer and this can change any time.
nebulous ever changing rules create uncertainty, stress and harm the market overall.

paragraphs.text: IMHO the current behavior by major payment processors should be given some attention as their erratic rule enforcement results in a lot of insecurity and affects marginalized groups. In the past years payment processors have setup a system they label as risk reduction. A "potential risk" can be a concerted phoning effort by small groups. This triggers a threat to annul the contract with the payment provider. Given that Mastercard / Visa have a high market share this can be seen as "Comply or close shop" and may even target completely legal sale of goods - or be enforced if only a minute amount of goods/services might break the forset rules. The contract partners have no choice, but to comply adding vague and often unclear terms of service. Partners of those platforms no longer have a clear guidance what they can and cannot offer and this can change any time. As cartel offices have allowed this market concentration, the freedom of doing buisnuis for the big companies should be curtailed. They should not be able to threaten smaller shops with random rule enforcement this only creates irritation and uncertainty. If there is clear evidence for illegal content these companies should use the legal authorities instead. This ensures that the law is upheld, products are judged by the same standard and creators / companies have clear guidelines to follow. In the past Patreon Creators, Sex workers, LGBTQ+ creators, adult content providers were all effected by random outbursts by the payment provider. US companies with their prudish tendencies should not have the power to impede and homogenize different subcultures with claimed business risks - where break-out groups weaponize payment providers as ultimate nukes to sanitize sprawling subcultures, legal services, art and games. Small businesses and self employed people using the platforms are faced with nebulous ever changing rules create uncertainty, stress and harm the market overall. The EU should make sure that there is an even playing ground even for small creators, members of minority group to create their own goods and services without the fear of erratic market bullies that got wind up by random phone calls in another continent. If we have payment few providers with a combined market share of 60-90% their conduct needs to be monitored and if there are signs of abuse of power and bullying - smaller platforms need a leg up for an equal stable environment to grow and flourish.
